mission.title: cert-manager: Sane defaults for Certificate revision history limit
mission.description: Sane defaults for Certificate revision history limit . Requested by 20+ users.

steps.title: Check current cert-manager deployment
steps.description:
Verify your cert-manager version and configuration:
```bash
kubectl get pods -n cert-manager -l app.kubernetes.io/name=cert-manager
helm list -n cert-manager 2>/dev/null || echo "Not installed via Helm"
```
This feature requires a working cert-manager installation.

steps.title: Review cert-manager configuration
steps.description:
Inspect the relevant cert-manager configuration:
```bash
kubectl get all -n cert-manager -l app.kubernetes.io/name=cert-manager
kubectl get configmap -n cert-manager -l app.kubernetes.io/part-of=cert-manager
```
yes. https://github.com/jetstack/cert-manager/pull/3773 introduced garbage collecting certificateRequest resources. But, the default revisionHistoryLimit is nil, which means revisions will be not be garbage collected, by default.

steps.title: Apply the fix for Sane defaults for Certificate revision history limit
steps.description:
It's not an ideal solution, but *if* any of you use Kyverno, my colleague  has been writing a tutorial which explains how to use a ClusterPolicy to apply sensible defaults to Certificates. PTAL:
 * https://github.com/cert-manager/website/pull/1279

See the source issue for community-verified solutions.

steps.title: Verify the feature works
steps.description:
Test that the new capability is working as expected:
```bash
kubectl get pods -n cert-manager -l app.kubernetes.io/name=cert-manager
kubectl get events -n cert-manager --sort-by='.lastTimestamp' | tail -10
```
Confirm the feature described in "Sane defaults for Certificate revision history limit " is functioning correctly.

resolution.summary:
It's not an ideal solution, but *if* any of you use Kyverno, my colleague  has been writing a tutorial which explains how to use a ClusterPolicy to apply sensible defaults to Certificates. PTAL:
 * https://github.com/cert-manager/website/pull/1279

prerequisites.description: A running Kubernetes cluster with cert-manager installed or the issue environment reproducible.
